scorer.name: claude-opus-4-6
scorer.notes: Initial baseline scoring for the deep eval harness. Performed as the end-to-end dry run required by cortex-0jm AC4. Judgments based on top-5 hits and per-retrieval module mix; eval/* artifacts are treated as over-retrieval contamination (see summary.notes).

verdicts.reasoning: Rank-1 is internal/datom/CLAUDE.md and rank-2 is docs/spec/cortex-spec.md; either body fully supports the gold answer on datom fields.

load_bearing.query: what is a datom and what fields does it contain

verdicts.reasoning: Expected module is in top-3 (cortex-spec.md at rank 3) but rank-1/2 are eval self-hits, not the activation module. Answer is recoverable but buried.

verdicts.reasoning: cortex-spec.md at rank 2 supports the four weights answer, but rank-1 is eval/questions.json which is over-retrieval.

summary.notes: Deep eval immediately surfaces two high-impact quality issues the lexical gate cannot see. (1) Eval self-recall: eval/results*.json, eval/questions.json, and eval/run_eval.sh dominate top-1 for 21/30 questions because they were ingested and their verbatim keyword lists are lexical magnets. They should be excluded via .cortexignore. (2) Negative queries never trigger empty — 0/5 — suggesting the composite activation score has no calibrated 'no relevance' floor; every query returns the same small set of eval artifacts regardless of topic. File both as separate beads; they were invisible to eval/run_eval.sh's keyword-match scoring.
